judgeReasoning: The response satisfies all required rubric points: it explains that `group` drives navigation, llms.txt sections, search metadata, and AGENTS.md grouping; it characterizes `/llms-full.txt` as the monolithic all-pages fallback not split by group; and it names multiple valid optional fields (icon, deprecated, experimental, canary, new, draft, tags, full, lastModified, lastAuthor). Minor concern: it invents some fields not in the rubric's list (deprecatedReason, availableIn) and claims per-group monolithic bundles exist at `/docs/llms-full/<group>.txt`, which somewhat contradicts the rubric's note that site mode no longer emits per-group full-context files by default — but the core required claim that `/llms-full.txt` itself is not sharded is clearly stated.
